title: Norms for Parish Pastoral Councils — Diocese of Pensacola-Tallahassee
summary: Establishes the bishop's norms for the nature, establishment, membership, and function of Parish Pastoral Councils in every parish of the Diocese of Pensacola-Tallahassee, in accord with canon 536.
notes: The document is formatted as Appendix 2 within a larger governing document; the full parent document is not provided, so chapter/section addressing is a best guess. The 'Rev 8/14' notation indicates a revision in August 2014, used as a proxy for last_review_date since no explicit review date is stated. The effective date (2004-01-21) is taken from the bishop's letter date. No retention period is stated or strongly implied; the minutes and by-laws produced by these councils would fall under Administrative Records (Permanent) per diocesan retention norms, but this policy document itself is a governing norm and would also likely be Permanent — however this cannot be confirmed from the text alone. Owner role is listed as Vicar General as a reasonable proxy for canonical/parish governance oversight; the Bishop's Office issued the norms but ongoing administration typically falls to the Vicar General. A human reviewer should confirm the correct chapter address within the diocesan policy manual and verify whether a more recent revision supersedes the Aug 2014 version.
